text.title: Puppeteer Launch & Navigation Setup Failure Modes
text.summary: 5 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Docker run fails with 'No usable sandbox! Update your kernel...' the first time it boots Chromium.
antipattern.fix: Add both flags to launch args. Keep them only in container/CI; do not propagate to local dev.

antipattern.symptom: Script hangs for 30s on every page.goto against the SPA dashboard.
antipattern.root-cause: Default networkidle0 never settles because WebSocket and analytics traffic keeps the network alive.
antipattern.fix: Default to waitUntil='domcontentloaded' + targeted waitForSelector on the element the next action needs.

antipattern.symptom: Server RAM usage grows after every job; ps shows multiple chrome --headless processes.
antipattern.root-cause: Script throws after newPage; browser.close was inside the try block, never reached.
antipattern.fix: Move browser.close into a finally block. Add process.on('SIGTERM') cleanup for daemon workers.

antipattern.symptom: Yesterday's CI passed; today's CI fails on a selector that exists on the page when opened manually.
antipattern.root-cause: Puppeteer auto-installed a new Chromium revision; default headless mode changed slightly.
antipattern.fix: Pin the revision in package.json or use @sparticuz/chromium at a fixed version; commit lockfile.

antipattern.symptom: Parent process kills the job because Puppeteer's 30s default + multiple waits exceed the 60s budget.
antipattern.root-cause: Script relied on default timeout; waits stacked unpredictably.
antipattern.fix: Call page.setDefaultTimeout(20000) (or per-call timeout) so each wait stays well below the parent budget.
